definition: A spending or consuming, often a disbursement of funds.
example: Buying the car was a big expense, but will be worth it in the long run.

definition: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.
example: The guard blew his whistle to indicate imminent departure.

definition: A pale yellowish (golden brown) color, especially said of hair color.
example: blond ale

definition: The act of one who proceeds, or who prosecutes a design or transaction
example: a cautious or a violent proceeding

definition: A person or thing who enjoys special regard or favour.
example: The teacher's favourite always went first.

definition: A distinguishing feature of a person or thing.
example: The characteristic of a field, if non-zero, must be a prime number.

definition: Disdainful indication that something is obvious.
To cause (something) to cease to be in one's possession or capability due to unfortunate or unknown circumstances, events or reasons.
example: He lost his hearing in the explosion.

definition: An organic compound.
example: The writing of the script was an organic process.

definition: The operation of navigating through a stream.

definition: The effect upon the judgment or feelings produced by any event, whether witnessed or participated in; personal and direct impressions as contrasted with description or fancies; personal acquaintance; actual enjoyment or suffering.
example: It was an experience he would not soon forget.

definition: A book specially designed to keep photographs, stamps, or autographs.
To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.

definition: (degree) To an extreme degree.
An agreement between two or more parties, to perform a specific job or work order, often temporary or of fixed duration and usually governed by a written agreement.
example: Marriage is a contract.

definition: A recipient of hospitality, specifically someone staying by invitation at the house of another.
example: The guests were let in by the butler.

definition: To perform the role of a host.
example: I was terrible at hosting that show.

definition: An abnormal condition of a human, animal or plant that causes discomfort or dysfunction; distinct from injury insofar as the latter is usually instantaneously acquired.
example: The tomato plants had some kind of disease that left their leaves splotchy and fruit withered.

definition: To relate or belong to; to have reference to or connection with; to affect the interest of; to be of importance to.
example: A good prince concerns himself in the happiness of his subjects.

definition: A person or entity engaged in the creation or improvement of certain classes of products.
Anything that is virtually equal to something else, or has the same value, force, etc.
example: To burn calories, a thirty-minute jog is equivalent to a couple of hamburgers.

definition: Stylish, high-toned, upscale.
The quality of being a neighbor, of living nearby, next to each-other; proximity.
example: Our neighborhood was our only reason to exchange hollow greetings.

definition: A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.

definition: Something that is variable.
example: There are several variables to consider here.

definition: Regardless; anyhow.
example: He didn't enjoy washing his car, but it was so dirty that he did it anyway.

definition: An option allowing a gamer to resume play after game over, when all lives have been lost.
example: Do you want me to continue to unload these?

definition: A mark left by something that has passed along.
example: Can you see any tracks in the snow?

definition: A turning or sliding piece which imparts motion to a rod, lever or block brought into sliding or rolling contact with it.
The set of courses, coursework, and their content, offered at a school or university.
A method of human thought that involves thinking in a linear, step-by-step manner about how a problem can be solved. Logic is the basis of many principles including the scientific method.
example: It's hard to work out his system of logic.

definition: A physical object whose shape is used as a guide to make other objects.
A (male) ruler, a sovereign; a king, monarch.
example: He is a prince among men.

definition: A two-dimensional geometric figure, a line, consisting of the set of all those points in a plane that are equally distant from a given point (center).
example: The set of all points (x, y) such that (x − 1)2 + y2 = r2 is a circle of radius r around the point (1, 0).

definition: A mixture of mineral particles and organic material, used to support plant growth.
example: Kenyan soil

definition: The act of granting; a bestowing or conferring; concession; allowance; permission.
example: I got a grant from the government to study archeology in Egypt.

definition: In or at any location or an unknown location.
example: I don't know where I left my keys. They could be anywhere.

definition: The study of the human mind.
An answer or reply, or something in the nature of an answer or reply.
Liquid or moisture.
example: Don't go out in the wet.

definition: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.
A person who invests money in order to make a profit.
A male sheep, typically uncastrated
To have a consequence or remnant.
example: We were not left go to the beach after school except on a weekend.

definition: An implement, an instrument or apparatus designed (or at least used) as a means to a specific end (often specified), especially:
(coppersmithing) An alloy of copper, tin, iron, etc.; white metal.
example: Flat or matte paint allows a deep color expression on the walls while also hiding flaws that may be inherent on the painted surface.

definition: An elementary school
To prepare (food) for eating by heating it, often by combining it with other ingredients.
example: I'm cooking bangers and mash.

definition: Used in speaking.
example: one's normal speaking voice

definition: A person or organisation with some sort of responsibility for another person or organisation, especially where the responsibility has a religious, legal, or financial aspect.
example: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

definition: A red fox, small carnivore (Vulpes vulpes), related to dogs and wolves, with red or silver fur and a bushy tail.
example: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

definition: A response.
example: to respond to a question or an argument

definition: (obsolete outside dialectal) An assize.

definition: Flat, level.
example: Throughout high school she worried that she had a rather plain face.

definition: To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.

definition: The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
example: She stood with her right arm extended and her palm forward to indicate “Stop!”

definition: An object designed to open and close a lock.
example: The key to solving this problem is persistence.

definition: The movement of a vessel from land into the water; especially, the sliding on ways from the stocks on which it is built. (Compare: to splash a ship.)
example: book launch

definition: To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.
example: The flag waved in the gentle breeze.

definition: To inspect; to examine.
example: Check the oil in your car once a month.

definition: A rib.
A digital image meant to be printed on paper.
A thing that is extremely holy; used almost exclusively in Holy of Holies.
example: I'm planning to visit the holy city of Jerusalem this Christmas.

definition: Something done, a deed.
example: an act of goodwill

definition: A structure made of connected strands of metal, fiber, or other flexible/ductile material, with evenly spaced openings between them.
example: The music meshed well with the visuals in that film.

definition: The track or indication marking the route followed by something that has passed, such as the footprints of animal on land or the contrail of an airplane in the sky.
example: The hunters trailed their prey deep into the woods.

definition: The act of enforcing; compulsion.
A character or glyph representing an idea, concept or object.
example: $ is the symbol for dollars in the US and some other countries.

definition: Strength; power; might; force .
example: The craft of writing plays.

definition: A friend or casual acquaintance.
example: They have been buddies since they were in school.

definition: A book with a rigid binding, often of cardboard or leather.
To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
example: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.

definition: A senior official in a college or university, who may be in charge of a division or faculty (for example, the dean of science) or have some other advisory or disciplinary function (for example, the dean of students).
Equipment designed for a particular purpose; an apparatus.
example: The laboratory included an elaborate setup for measuring the energy.

definition: A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.
example: The student council had a poll to see what people want served in the cafeteria.

definition: To reserve (something) for future use.
example: I can book tickets for the concert next week.

definition: A list of terms in a particular domain of knowledge with their definitions.
Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.
Any Unix-like operating system, including Unix.
To occupy fully, to take up all of.
example: the bucket filled with rain;  the sails fill with wind

definition: Evidence of a long-term debt, by which the bond issuer (the borrower) is obliged to pay interest when due, and repay the principal at maturity, as specified on the face of the bond certificate. The rights of the holder are specified in the bond indenture, which contains the legal terms and conditions under which the bond was issued. Bonds are available in two forms: registered bonds, and bearer bonds.
example: Investors face a quandary. Cash offers a return of virtually zero in many developed countries; government-bond yields may have risen in recent weeks but they are still unattractive. Equities have suffered two big bear markets since 2000 and are wobbling again. It is hardly surprising that pension funds, insurers and endowments are searching for new sources of return.
